mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate above 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which reduces corporate earnings visibility and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and increase in inflation expectations (CPIAUCSL) signals stagflationary pressures that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 60-day returns in stagflation scenarios are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 5% signals stronger consumer demand and corporate investment, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of triggered instances during expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2% signals central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window or 60-day returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below -100k signals weak labor market performance, which reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in real GDP (GDPC1) above 3% signals strong economic growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 60-day returns in growth scenarios are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 0.5% signals weakening supply-side capacity, which reduces corporate profits and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal.
